taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the temporal and infratemporal regions.
textField:
The four muscles of *mastication* are the *masseter*, *temporalis*, *medial* *pterygoid*, and *lateral* *pterygoid*. All are supplied by the *mandibular* division (V3) of the *trigeminal* nerve. The masseter and temporalis are powerful *elevators* of the mandible. The lateral pterygoid is the only muscle that *depresses* and *protrudes* the mandible.

The *maxillary* artery is the terminal branch of the *external* *carotid* artery and is the dominant vessel of the *infratemporal* fossa. It gives off the *middle* *meningeal* artery, which enters the skull through the *foramen* *spinosum*. The *inferior* *alveolar* artery supplies the mandibular teeth. The *pterygoid* *venous* plexus communicates with the *cavernous* sinus, providing a route for spread of *dental* infections *intracranially*.

The *temporomandibular* joint (TMJ) is a *synovial* joint with an *articular* disc dividing it into upper and lower compartments. In TMJ *dislocation*, the *condyle* slips anterior to the articular *eminence* and is locked by spasm of the lateral pterygoid.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review temporal and infratemporal anatomy terminology.
Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key anatomical terms accurately.
